parsed_review.summary: CP00-445 (Risk C, 150 units, RP14.P06.M06.S07-RP14.P07.M02.S15) is a descriptor-only settlement-core pack that closes RP14.P06 (M06 tail through M10) and opens the RP14.P07 foundation (M00-M02 head). Read/Grep/Glob inspection confirms the pack-level claim is supported and honest: manifest declares production_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, runtime_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review; the Hermes packet only sets production_ready_candidate (gated on coverage+slice validity), never a hard production_ready. Counts reconcile to 150 across phase (102+48), micro-phase (16/22/22/22/20/13/20/15), and deliverable groups in registry requirements, plan pack, contract, and the validator coverage summary. Implementation (createSettlementCoreCp445* in service.js/validators.js) returns Object.freeze'd results with full no-write/no-real-data attestations; the descriptor validator enforces descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false per row. model.test.js (3 CP445 tests) and scripts/validate-rp14-settlement-core-contract.mjs assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence-packet gates, and the CP00-446 handoff; command-evidence.json records exit 0 for node --test, npm test, npm run validate, rp14:settlement-core:validate, fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane/weighted/spec:requirements:validate, and git diff --check. Authority boundary is preserved everywhere: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, Claude review packet read_only=true with allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob. Hermes patterns are confined to deterministic descriptor evidence packet/receipt metadata (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). CP178+ hardening is satisfied: forbidden_review_evidence covers failed_authentication_run, zero_byte_output, malformed_json_output, fenced_json_output, tool_call_shaped_output, disabled_tools_run, missing_raw_output_reference, missing_source_inspection_basis, claude_final_approval_claim, source_mutation_claim, and production_enterprise_trust_claim; readiness auth_preflight passed (1248-byte valid JSON, exit 0, non-zero stdout). Sequencing is correct: raw-output.json, review-receipt.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json are absent because this read-only review is the gating step that produces/precedes them. Unrelated dirty files (the v2.0 docx, workbook artifacts, ldip invalid attempt) are correctly excluded from active_pack_implementation_refs. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; two P3 informational notes only. This review does not block pack or goal closeout and is not final approval authority.

findings.title: Manifest references post-review artifacts not yet present (expected by hardening sequence)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts and evidence_refs list docs/closeout-packs/cp00-445/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, which do not yet exist (Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-445/ returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json). This is consistent with the current hardening step: pack_level_claude_review.status=pending and valid_review_receipts=[]. The absent files are produced downstream of this read_only_claude_run gate (normalize -> receipt_validate -> finding_adjudication -> construction_inspection -> final_closeout_validation). Not a defect in the implementation under review.
findings.recommendation: At final closeout, ensure npm run closeout-pack:validate confirms claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json exist and validate before commit. No change required to the CP00-445 implementation for this review to pass.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json dual-lists pack-relevant untracked paths under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-445/ appear in both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The cp00-445/ directory is genuinely this pack's artifact location, so labeling it 'unrelated' is cosmetically inconsistent. This does not cause misattribution because the authoritative pack-scope list (active_pack_implementation_refs) cleanly contains only the 8 settlement source files, and the manifest's own worktree_transition_policy.unrelated_dirty_files_preserved correctly omits these two paths. A generator categorization quirk in the scope file only.
findings.recommendation: Optionally tighten the changed-file-scope generator so pack-owned untracked paths are not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No impact on the CP00-445 production_ready claim or boundaries.
